tasks.subject: [pip-panel] [P1] Build the PipHost content-routing feature
tasks.description:
Create the content host feature at `apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel/` (`ui/PipHost.tsx`, `ui/DemoPipContent.tsx`, `index.ts`), mirroring how `features/canvas` routes `UiCanvasContent` variants.

`PipHost` responsibilities:
- Read `pipContent`, `pipGeometry`, `closePip`, `setPipGeometry` from `useAppStore` (`@/layers/shared/model`).
- Render `null` when `pipContent` is `null` (cheap early return â€” nothing renders when there is nothing to show).
- Mobile guard first (ideation D2): call `useIsMobile()` (`@/layers/shared/model`, 768px breakpoint); when `true`, render `null`. Pair with a `useEffect` keyed on the mobile flag that calls `closePip()` whenever `isMobile` becomes `true` while `pipContent` is not already `null` â€” this is what makes crossing the breakpoint while a panel is open close it automatically instead of leaving stale content behind an invisible host.
- Default geometry: when `pipGeometry` is `null`, compute a bottom-right dock â€” `{ x: window.innerWidth - 360 - 16, y: window.innerHeight - 240 - 16, width: 360, height: 240 }` (a 360Ă—240 panel, 16px margins from both edges) â€” recomputed on every render while ungeometried (nothing is written to the store until the user's first drag/resize commits a geometry, so a window resize before that first gesture keeps the dock pinned to the corner instead of going stale).
- Renderer map: a MODULE-SCOPE constant declared at the top of `PipHost.tsx`, outside the component function â€” never an inline object literal or a `useMemo` result created inside the component body:

```tsx
const PIP_RENDERERS: { demo: React.ComponentType<{ content: Extract<PipContent, { kind: 'demo' }> }> } = {
  demo: DemoPipContent,
};
```

This mirrors the fix documented in `apps/client/src/layers/features/chat/ui/message/StreamingText.tsx:33-70` (the `FenceContext`/`DorkosUiFence` pair): that file's TSDoc explains that a renderer component recreated as a fresh closure on every render gets a fresh identity, which React treats as a different component type and unmounts/remounts the whole subtree â€” for PIP this matters the moment DOR-298 puts a live, stateful widget inside; a remount on every parent re-render would destroy its in-flight state. `DemoPipContent` (new file, `ui/DemoPipContent.tsx`) is intentionally trivial: it takes `{ content: { kind: 'demo'; title: string } }` and renders the title centered in the panel body â€” used only by the playground showcase (task 3.1) and tests.
- Content routing: switch on `pipContent.kind` to pick the component from `PIP_RENDERERS` and render it with `content={pipContent}`. v1's `PipContent` union only has `'demo'`, so the switch has exactly one case plus an exhaustiveness guard (e.g. a `default` branch that asserts the remaining type is `never`) so adding `'mcp_app'`/`'widget'` in DOR-297/298 becomes a compile error until their case is added.
- `onRestore`: `undefined` for `'demo'` in v1 (no restore target exists for the playground content) â€” add a TSDoc comment noting DOR-297 wires this for "send back to inline/canvas" per ideation decision D8.
- Wire `FloatingPanel`'s `onGeometryChange` prop directly to the store's `setPipGeometry` action â€” because task 1.1's primitive only calls `onGeometryChange` once, at gesture end, with the final clamped geometry, this direct wiring alone satisfies "persist on pointerup, not per-frame" without `PipHost` needing any throttling of its own.
- Wire `onClose` to `closePip`.
- Title: `pipContent.title` (the only kind in v1 already carries a `title` field; future kinds must too).

Barrel `index.ts`:
```ts
/**
 * PIP panel host â€” routes serializable PipContent descriptors to their
 * renderer inside the shared floating-panel primitive. The content-blind
 * primitive and the typed content union live in shared/ui and shared/model;
 * this feature is the only place that knows how each `kind` renders (mirrors
 * features/canvas routing UiCanvasContent).
 *
 * @module features/pip-panel
 */
export { PipHost } from './ui/PipHost';
```

Unit tests at `apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel/__tests__/PipHost.test.tsx` (mock `useAppStore` and `useIsMobile` per `.claude/rules/testing.md`'s Mock Browser APIs pattern):
- renders nothing when `pipContent` is `null`
- renders the `demo` content's title when `pipContent` is `{ kind: 'demo', title: '...' }`
- uses the computed bottom-right default geometry when `pipGeometry` is `null` (assert the rendered position/size matches the 360Ă—240/16px-margin formula)
- when `useIsMobile()` returns `true` and `pipContent` is non-null, the host renders nothing AND calls `closePip()` (mock it and assert it fired)
- renderer identity is stable across re-renders: render `PipHost` with open demo content, force a parent re-render (change an unrelated prop or context value), and assert the demo content's mount effect (a spy inside a `useEffect(() => { spy(); return cleanupSpy; }, [])` in `DemoPipContent`, or an equivalent mount-counting technique) fires exactly once â€” proving no remount happened.

tasks.subject: [pip-panel] [P1] Mount PipHost in both shells and prove it survives route changes
tasks.description:
Mount the host at the singleton tail of both client shells, outside the router-swapped subtree, so a floating panel survives navigation.

- `apps/client/src/AppShell.tsx`: import `{ PipHost }` from `@/layers/features/pip-panel` and add `<PipHost />` to the singleton tail after `<Toaster />` (currently lines 363-367: `<DialogHost />`, `<CommandPaletteDialog />`, `<CreateAgentDialog />`, `<ShortcutsPanel />`, `<Toaster />`) â€” placed there, OUTSIDE the `<PanelGroup>` (lines 349-354) that wraps `<Outlet />`, so it never remounts on navigation.
- `apps/client/src/App.tsx` (Obsidian embedded shell, no router at all): same import, `<PipHost />` added to its singleton tail after `<Toaster />` (currently lines 154-156: `<CommandPaletteDialog />`, `<ShortcutsPanel />`, `<Toaster />`).
- Acceptance: `grep -rn "react-router\|tanstack/react-router" apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/floating-panel.tsx apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store/app-store-pip.ts` returns nothing â€” confirms no router import reached the primitive, slice, or host (required for the Obsidian embedded shell).

Integration test at `apps/client/src/__tests__/pip-panel-route-persistence.test.tsx`, following the mocking style of the existing `apps/client/src/__tests__/app-shell-slots.test.tsx` (mocks `@tanstack/react-router`'s `useRouterState`/`Outlet` via a module-level `mockPathname` variable, mocks the sibling shell children that aren't relevant here, wraps in `QueryClientProvider` + `TransportProvider` with `createMockTransport()` from `@dorkos/test-utils`, and mocks `window.matchMedia`):
- render `<AppShell />` once via RTL's `render()`
- open PIP content through the real store (`useAppStore.getState().openPip({ kind: 'demo', title: 'Route test' })`) so `PipHost` renders a panel with `role="complementary"`
- capture the panel's DOM node via `screen.getByRole('complementary')`
- change `mockPathname` (e.g. `/` â†’ `/session`) and call the SAME render result's `rerender(...)` (not a fresh `render()`/`cleanup()` cycle â€” that would prove nothing about persistence) with the same `<AppShell />` element
- assert the panel's DOM node is the SAME node reference as before (`screen.getByRole('complementary')` after the rerender `===` the captured node) and its title text is unchanged â€” proving `PipHost` never unmounted across the simulated navigation, because it sits outside the `<Outlet/>`/`<PanelGroup>` subtree that a route change actually re-renders.
tasks.activeForm: Mounting PipHost in both shells and proving it survives route changes

tasks.subject: [pip-panel] [P2] Add the PIP Panel Dev Playground showcase
tasks.description:
Add a showcase so the primitive is fully verifiable before either accepted consumer (DOR-297, DOR-298) exists.

- New file `apps/client/src/dev/showcases/PipPanelShowcases.tsx`, following the `PlaygroundSection` + `ShowcaseDemo` wrapper pattern used by `apps/client/src/dev/showcases/OverlayShowcases.tsx` (the Dialog/Sheet/Popover showcases).
- Because the Dev Playground (`apps/client/src/dev/DevPlayground.tsx`) never mounts `AppShell`/`App.tsx`, this showcase must mount `<PipHost />` itself, once, inside the showcase component â€” otherwise `openPip` calls have nothing to render into.
- Controls exercising the full interaction contract this primitive ships (open at a persisted-or-default position, drag anywhere, resize from the corner, replace-on-reopen, close): a button that calls `useAppStore.getState().openPip({ kind: 'demo', title: 'Demo panel A' })`, a second button with a different title (proves replace-on-open â€” single instance, no stacking), and a close button calling `closePip()`. Add instructional copy near the controls telling the tester to drag the header and resize from the bottom-right corner to check clamping, and to reload the `/dev` page afterward to see the position survive (geometry persists via localStorage exactly like the production panel).
- Register the showcase: add `PlaygroundSection` entries (one per demonstrated behavior, at the granularity already used by the existing `FEATURES_SECTIONS` entries) to `apps/client/src/dev/sections/features-sections.ts` with `page: 'features'`, a new `category: 'PIP Panel'`, and `keywords` covering `['pip', 'panel', 'floating', 'drag', 'resize', 'mini-player', 'pop out']`. Import and render `<PipPanelShowcases />` inside `apps/client/src/dev/pages/FeaturesPage.tsx` alongside the other `*Showcases` components (reusing the existing Features page avoids adding a new `Page` union member, route, and sidebar nav entry for a single showcase group â€” consistent with how Relay's several showcase files already share that one page).
- No new automated tests required here â€” there is no end-user entry point in v1, so this showcase IS the verification surface until DOR-297/298 add real affordances. Still confirm `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` passes and, running `pnpm dev:dogfood`, that visiting `/dev/features` shows the new "PIP Panel" section in the TOC/search and every control behaves as described with no console errors.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the PIP Panel Dev Playground showcase

tasks.subject: [pip-panel] [P2] Write the changelog fragment for the PIP panel primitive
tasks.description:
Add a changelog fragment for this developer-visible surface even though DOR-296 ships no end-user-facing behavior yet (DOR-297/298 carry the docs and changelog entries for the visible behavior their affordances add later).

- New file `changelog/unreleased/<id>-pip-panel-primitive.md` where `<id>` is a FRESH `YYMMDD-HHMMSS` timestamp id minted with `.claude/scripts/id.ts` at write time (coordination-free per ADR-0312) â€” do not reuse this spec's own id `260711-150300`.
- No YAML frontmatter â€” plain Markdown, following the exact convention already in `changelog/unreleased/260711-105140-marketplace-symlink-containment.md`: a single `###` category heading (`Added`, since this ships a new primitive), followed by one bullet ending in a tracker reference in parens (`(DOR-296)`).
- Write the bullet for a developer audience per the `writing-for-humans` skill: plain language, no jargon for jargon's sake, no em dashes, describing what exists now rather than internal implementation. Example shape, write your own wording rather than copying this: "Added a reusable floating panel: a draggable, resizable mini-window that can stay open while you move around the app, remembering where you left it. Nothing uses it yet; it's the foundation for popping things like MCP apps and interactive widgets out of the main view in upcoming releases (DOR-296)."
tasks.activeForm: Writing the changelog fragment for the PIP panel primitive

tasks.subject: [pip-panel] [P2] Final accessibility and design-token review, then full verify
tasks.description:
Close out the PR with the polish pass and the full verification loop.

- Design-token audit against `contributing/design-system.md` (elevation/motion/mobile-scale sections) and `.claude/rules/components.md`: confirm `floating-panel.tsx` uses only the Calm Tech tokens already in play elsewhere â€” `shadow-floating`, `bg-card`, `border-border`, `rounded-lg`, `size-(--size-icon-sm)` on icon controls, 100-300ms animation durations â€” with no ad hoc hex colors, no `Math.random()`, and no bare `focus:` on interactive controls (must be `focus-visible:` per the Accessibility rules in `.claude/rules/components.md`).
- Accessibility pass beyond the unit tests already written in tasks 1.1/2.1: drive the Dev Playground showcase (task 3.1) with a keyboard and a screen reader or axe DevTools, confirming the close/restore controls are reachable via Tab with visible focus rings. Drag/resize has no keyboard-only equivalent in v1 â€” note that as a known limitation if it comes up in review rather than silently adding scope.
- Mobile-scale check: at a real ~375px viewport (or DevTools device emulation), confirm a panel open above 768px closes the instant the viewport crosses below 768px (the mobile guard from task 2.1), and that nothing renders below 768px (there is no pop-out affordance yet in v1, so this is confirming absence, not a new affordance check).
- Run the full verification loop and fix anything red: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck`, `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client lint`, `pnpm vitest run apps/client/src/layers/shared/ui/__tests__/floating-panel.test.tsx apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store/__tests__/app-store-pip.test.ts apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel/__tests__/PipHost.test.tsx apps/client/src/__tests__/pip-panel-route-persistence.test.tsx`, then the full `pnpm verify` (affected-only typecheck+lint+test) before opening the PR. Never substitute a bare `pnpm vitest run` for the full-suite check â€” it falsely fails two unrelated tests in the dev environment; use `pnpm test -- --run` for that.
- Confirm no lingering TODOs, no console warnings from the new components, and that every export in `apps/client/src/layers/features/pip-panel/`, `floating-panel.tsx`, and `app-store-pip.ts` carries TSDoc, including the module-level `@module` doc blocks.
tasks.activeForm: Running the final accessibility and design-token review, then full verify
